creator: toyonaga@msd.ts.fujitsu.co.jp
text:
Hi, 
First of all, Thank you for answer.

I made a big mistake when submitting pr. I forgot to mention that
the version of MH is jp-patched one. But you seem to catch that point.

I currently use(output from inc -help):

version: MH 6.8.3.JP2c #1[UCI] (flux.msd.ts.fujitsu.co.jp) of Wed May 28 12:16:00 JST 1997
options: [APOP='"/usr/local/etc/pop.auth"'] [ATHENA] [BIND] [BPOP]
         [BSD42] [BSD43] [BSD44] [DBMPWD] [DUMB] [FOLDPROT='"0700"']
         [JAPAN] [MHE] [MHRC] [MIME] [MORE='"/usr/local/bin/less"']
         [MPOP] [NORUSERPASS] [NTOHLSWAP] [OVERHEAD] [POP]
         [POPSERVICE='"pop3"'] [POSIX] [RPATHS] [RPOP] [SBACKUP='"#"']
         [SENDMTS] [SENDMTS] [SMTP] [SMTP] [SYS5DIR] [TYPESIG=void]
         [UNISTD] [VSPRINTF] [WAITINT] [WHATNOW] [ZONEINFO]



From: MIHIRA "Sanpei" Yoshiro <sanpei@yy.cs.keio.ac.jp>
Subject: Re: ports/4458: MH's packf command dumps core
Date: Sun, 14 Sep 1997 16:20:01 +0900

>   One solution is that ja-mh would use new japaneized MH patch(*1):
> 
> ftp://ftp.tut.ac.jp:/Linux/JE-0.9.8a/sources/net/mh-6.8.3+-jp2c+-1.02.patch.gz
> 

I once tried that, but it made no difference.


						Regards,
						toyonaga@msd.ts.fujitsu.co.jp
